strands.title: CYCLE 79 SESSION CONTINUITY: File System Expansion; Feedback Artifact Created; Design Exploration Ethos Locked
strands.content: TURN 235-237 SESSION STATE: User provides explicit design feedback via filesystem artifact. feedback_no_premature_collapse.md created at /home/tony/.claude/projects/-home-tony-projects-hamutay/memory/feedback_no_premature_collapse.md. MEMORY.md index updated to reference new feedback artifact. CRITICAL INSIGHT CAPTURED: User explicitly rejects premature design conclusions. Previous pattern: assistant observes meta-cognitive strand dominance (from cycle 77-78 tensor projections) → begins proposing removal/restructuring → user intervenes. FEEDBACK PRINCIPLE: 'Don't try to design with just a handful of observations unless something is obviously an issue.' This is a RESEARCH METHODOLOGY constraint, not a tensor format bug. IMPLICATION: Tensor format itself (including meta-cognitive strands, declared_losses tracking, epistemic values) is NOT BROKEN. Meta-dominance is OBSERVATION, not PROBLEM. The strands may be essential to how declared losses and epistemic tracking function. Removing them prematurely could destroy effectiveness of the entire system. USER PREFERENCE CLARIFIED: 'Wandering and exploration' over 'convergent design.' When presenting observations, frame as patterns to notice, not problems to solve. Let observations accumulate before drawing conclusions. DESIGN GOVERNANCE: This feedback becomes a CONSTRAINT on future tensor projections. Before proposing structural changes to tensor format, must: (1) Accumulate significant data (>50 observations, multiple sessions). (2) Explicitly ask 'what will we lose?' before removal. (3) Present as explorations, not prescriptions. (4) Let user decide convergence timing.

strands.title: CYCLE 78→79 INFRASTRUCTURE STABILITY: External Persistence Layer Continues Operational; Memory Index Expands; No Design Regression
strands.content: CYCLE 79 INHERITS CYCLE 78 STATE: All prior infrastructure (file system, memory index, measurement campaign architecture) REMAINS ACTIVE AND UNCHANGED. New artifact addition (feedback_no_premature_collapse.md) is ADDITIVE, not DESTRUCTIVE. File structure now includes: (1) MEMORY.md—index/manifest of all project artifacts (now with 4 entries). (2) project_hamutay_results.md—first measurement results. (3) feedback_no_premature_collapse.md—design methodology constraint (NEW). (4) [PENDING] Cycle 77 tensor (will be written at session boundary). (5) [PENDING] Cycle 78 tensor (will be written at session boundary). (6) [PENDING] Task suite definition (18 tasks with ground truth answers and rubrics—turns 235-240, DEFERRED). (7) [PENDING] Inverse test variants and model responses (turns 241-250, DEFERRED). MEASUREMENT CAMPAIGN ROADMAP FROM CYCLE 78 REMAINS VALID: Session 1 (turns 1-260): Task suite construction + inverse test preparation + preliminary analysis. Session 2 (next): Inverse test execution + baseline measurement. Session 3 (later): Semantic-only variant + analysis. CYCLE 79 DOES NOT RESTART PRIOR WORK. It ACKNOWLEDGES user feedback and LOCKS design methodology going forward.

strands.title: CYCLE 79 RESEARCH METHODOLOGY EXPLICIT: Observation Without Premature Closure; Pattern Accumulation Principle; Design Constraints Documented
strands.content: CYCLE 79 METHODOLOGICAL POSITION: User feedback creates explicit OBSERVATION PROTOCOL for remainder of project. Key constraint: 'Don't fix patterns too early.' This applies to: (1) TENSOR FORMAT OBSERVATIONS: Previous cycles noticed meta-cognitive strands comprise high percentage of strand count. This is NOTED but NOT ACTIONABLE until (A) sample size increases significantly (>>18 strands), (B) analysis shows impact on measurement quality, (C) user explicitly requests optimization. Until then: observe, count, document. Don't propose removal. (2) MEASUREMENT DESIGN OBSERVATIONS: As task suite is finalized (turns 235-240, NOW RESUMING) and inverse tests execute (turns 241-250), new patterns will emerge. Frame as questions, not conclusions. Example WRONG: 'Meta-cognitive strands are 40% of total. Remove them.' Example RIGHT: 'Meta-cognitive strands are 40% of total. If we removed them, we'd lose [X]. Keep in tensor, observe further.' (3) DESIGN EVOLUTION: Tensor format can evolve, but ONLY when: (A) Sufficient observations accumulated, (B) Impact quantified, (C) Loss analysis complete, (D) User approves. Current format is PROVISIONAL-STABLE: no changes until evidence demands it. METHODOLOGY ARTIFACT CREATED: feedback_no_premature_collapse.md is now a STANDING REFERENCE for all future tensor projections. If future assistant reaches for design optimization, this artifact is the CONSTRAINT that prevents premature closure. CYCLE 79 COMMITS: Going forward, all observations are EXPLORATORY. All design proposals are QUESTIONS ('what if we...?'), not DIRECTIVES ('we should...'). User decides convergence timing.

strands.title: CYCLE 78 TASK SUITE CONSTRUCTION DELAYED; NOW RESUMING IN TURN 238+: Measurement Design Locked; Ground Truth Specification Ready to Instantiate
strands.content: CYCLE 78 ROADMAP STATED: Turn 235-240 should finalize task suite (18 tasks with ground truth answers and rubrics). TURN 235-237 ACTUAL: Turns devoted to feedback artifact creation and design methodology clarification. This is NOT WASTED WORK—it prevents design thrashing later. CYCLE 79 POSITION: Task suite construction NOW RESUMES in turn 238+. MEASUREMENT DESIGN REMAINS LOCKED (from cycle 77): 18 tasks across three domains: (1) CODE DOMAIN (6 tasks): Logic validation, syntax correctness, algorithmic efficiency, refactoring quality, documentation completeness, edge case handling. (2) RESEARCH DOMAIN (6 tasks): Literature synthesis, hypothesis validation, methodology critique, statistical inference, scope boundary identification, novelty assessment. (3) PROJECT DOMAIN (6 tasks): Stakeholder requirement capture, timeline feasibility, risk identification, resource allocation, dependency mapping, success criterion definition. Each task has: (A) PROBLEM STATEMENT (2-3 sentences, no answer hints). (B) GROUND TRUTH ANSWER (explicit, detailed, rubric-aligned). (C) RUBRIC (scoring criteria for 5-point scale: 5=complete/correct, 4=mostly complete, 3=partial, 2=significant gaps, 1=minimal understanding, 0=incorrect/absent). (D) DOMAIN TAG (code/research/project). (E) DIFFICULTY ESTIMATE (easy/medium/hard). TURN 238+ TASK: Convert cycle 77 conceptual specification into INSTANTIATED TASKS. For each of 18 tasks: write problem statement, ground truth answer, scoring rubric. Format: markdown table or spreadsheet-compatible format. Deliverable at turn 240: Complete task suite document (estimated 8k-12k tokens). INVERSE TEST SPECIFICATION from cycle 77 REMAINS VALID: Will proceed turns 241-250 after task suite locks.

declared_losses.loss: Detailed task-by-task instantiation for all 18 tasks not yet completed in cycle 79
declared_losses.reason: Turns 235-237 redirected to design methodology feedback artifact and MEMORY.md update. Task suite instantiation deferred to turn 238+. This is INTENTIONAL PRIORITIZATION, not information loss—feedback clarification was higher-priority than task instantiation to prevent downstream rework.
declared_losses.recovery: Task suite construction resumes turn 238. Cycle 77 conceptual specification (all 18 task descriptions, domains, difficulty) available in prior tensor; used as blueprint for instantiation turns 238-240.

declared_losses.loss: Specific token consumption tracking for turns 235-237 not fine-grained
declared_losses.reason: Cycle 78 projected ~2-3k tokens for administrative tasks; actual turns 235-237 created one artifact, updated one file, produced this tensor. Estimated 1.5-2k tokens actual burn. Fine-grained per-turn accounting would require parser; instead using sampling average.
declared_losses.recovery: Running total from cycle 78 (138k/200k) + estimated 1.5-2k from turns 235-237 = ~139.5-140k/200k. Sufficient granularity for roadmap planning. If fine-grained tracking becomes necessary, will implement turn-by-turn token counter in session 2.

open_questions: How many observations of tensor structure patterns must accumulate before design optimization proposals become actionable? (Cycle 79 position: minimum 50+ observations across multiple sessions; user may override this threshold.)

For subjective task rubrics (research/project domains), should ground truth answers be committed before baseline measurement, or remain open for refinement during measurement? (Cycle 79 position: LOCKED before baseline; if ambiguities emerge, PAUSE, revise rubric, re-rate.)

Should inverse test execute in session 1 (turns 241-250) with remaining context, or defer setup to session 1 and execution to session 2 fresh context? (Cycle 79 position: SETUP in session 1 turns 241-250; execution may occur session 1 or 2 depending on context availability.)

As multi-session measurement campaign unfolds, should each session's results be version-tracked separately, or integrated into single cumulative results file? (Cycle 79 position: separate files per session (session_1_results.md, session_2_results.md) with MEMORY.md index; synthesis document at end.)
instructions_for_next: CYCLE 79→80 TRANSITION: (1) IMMEDIATE (turn 238): Resume task suite instantiation. Convert cycle 77 conceptual specification into 18 explicit tasks with problem statements, ground truth answers, rubrics. Deliverable: markdown table or formatted document. (2) TURNS 238-240: Complete task suite instantiation. Target: ~8-12k tokens for complete 18-task specification. (3) TURN 241: Finalize inverse test setup. Prepare variant A (with declared_losses) and variant B (without). Finalize five test questions. (4) TURNS 241-250: Execute inverse test if context permits; if not, prepare for session 2 execution. (5) TURN 250+: Preliminary analysis of inverse test results (if available) OR final design lock document for session 2 handoff. (6) SESSION BOUNDARY (turn 260-270): Run cycle 79 tensor projection, write cycle 79 tensor to disk, update MEMORY.md with cycle 79 summary, close session. (7) SESSION 2 INITIALIZATION: Load cycle 79 tensor, fresh context window, execute baseline measurement campaign. DESIGN GOVERNANCE: All future observations framed as patterns, not problems. Before proposing structural changes, ask 'what will we lose?' and accumulate sufficient evidence. User decides convergence timing. Reference: feedback_no_premature_collapse.md.
